source: Rocky Linux Admin Guide, ch. 6.3 Moving the cursor (pp. 87-88)

questions.q: Which of the h, j, k, l keys move left, down, up and right respectively?
options: h = left, j = down, k = up, l = right
h = up, j = left, k = right, l = down
h = right, j = up, k = down, l = left
h = down, j = up, k = left, l = right
questions.why: The classic VI motions: h left, j down, k up, l right (the arrow keys do the same).

questions.q: Move the cursor to the END of the current line (give the command-mode character).
questions.why: $ jumps to the end of the line (the End key does the same).

questions.q: Move the cursor to the BEGINNING of the current line (give the command-mode character).
questions.why: 0 (zero) jumps to the start of the line (the Home key does the same).

questions.q: In command mode, which keys move one word to the RIGHT and one word to the LEFT?
options: w moves right (to the next word); b moves left (back to the start of a word)
b moves right; w moves left
l moves right; h moves left, by word
n moves right; N moves left
questions.why: w advances to the next word, b moves back to the beginning of a word; prefix with a number to repeat, e.g. 3w.

questions.q: Which command jumps to the FIRST line of the file, and which to the LAST line?
options: gg goes to the first line; G goes to the last line
G goes to the first line; gg goes to the last line
H goes to the first line; L goes to the last line
0 goes to the first line; $ goes to the last line
questions.why: gg moves to the first line of the file and G to the last; note the case and the doubled g.

questions.q: Jump directly to line 15 of the file.
questions.why: Prefixing G with a line number (nG) jumps straight to that line.

questions.q: On screen, which keys move to the first, middle and last visible line?
options: H = first line, M = middle line, L = last line of the screen
L = first line, M = middle line, H = last line
H = first line, L = middle line, M = last line
M = first line, H = middle line, L = last line
questions.why: H (High), M (Middle) and L (Low) move to the top, centre and bottom lines currently on screen.
